story.iWant: a properly configured .NET 9 WinUI 3 desktop application project
story.soThat: I have a working Windows desktop app shell to build features upon

tasks.task: Create WinUI 3 solution with .NET 9 Create new WinUI 3 App project using Visual Studio 2022 or dotnet CLI Verify target framework is net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 or later Verify Windows App SDK 1.5+ is referenced Test compilation succeeds with no errors

Establish project structure with folder organization Create /Models folder in project root Create /Views folder in project root Create /ViewModels folder in project root Create /Services folder in project root Create /Data folder in project root Verify folder structure matches architecture document

Configure dependency injection in App.xaml.cs Add Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection NuGet package Create ServiceCollection in App.xaml.cs OnLaunched Register placeholder service (e.g., ILogger) to verify DI works Verify services can be resolved in MainWindow

Set up development configuration files Create .editorconfig with C# formatting rules (4 spaces, UTF-8, CRLF for Windows) Create/update .gitignore for .NET, Visual Studio, WinUI 3 (bin/, obj/, .vs/, data/) Verify files are properly excluded from git tracking Commit configuration files to repository

Create basic MainWindow with placeholder UI Update MainWindow.xaml with Grid layout Add TextBlock with "Google Calendar Management - Loading..." placeholder text Set window default size to 1024x768 Set MinWidth="800" MinHeight="600" on Window element Verify window launches with correct size and constraints

Create GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests project Add xUnit test project to solution (.NET 9) Install NuGet packages: xunit, xunit.runner.visualstudio, FluentAssertions, Moq Add project reference to GoogleCalendarManagement project Create /Unit and /Integration folders in test project Verify test project compiles successfully

Write sample smoke test to verify test framework Create SmokeTests.cs in /Unit folder Write simple test: CanCreateMainWindow_ReturnsTrue() Run test and verify it passes in Visual Studio Test Explorer Verify test discovery works (test appears in Test Explorer)

Enable hot reload for faster development Verify Hot Reload is enabled in Visual Studio project properties Test hot reload by changing XAML TextBlock text while app running Verify change appears without restarting application

Validate final project setup Build solution in Release configuration - verify no errors Build solution in Debug configuration - verify no errors Launch application and verify window appears correctly Run all tests and verify they pass Verify project structure matches architecture document layout

criterion.when: I create the project using .NET 9 and Windows App SDK
criterion.then: the application compiles and launches with a basic window

criterion.condition: the project structure follows .NET best practices:

requirements.requirement: Proper folder organization (Models, Views, ViewModels, Services, Data)
Target framework set to net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 or later
Application launches to 1024x768 default window size
Window is resizable with min-width/min-height constraints
xUnit test project configured with FluentAssertions, Moq packages
Test project can discover and run tests successfully
Sample smoke test verifies test framework works

artifact.title: Epic 1: Foundation & Project Setup
artifact.snippet: Establishes .NET 9 + WinUI 3 project structure. Testing framework established in first story with xUnit, FluentAssertions, Moq. Use Visual Studio 2022 with Windows App SDK 1.5+. Enable hot reload for faster development iterations.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Create WinUI 3 project with .NET 9 using dotnet CLI or Visual Studio 2022. Establishes base architecture with WinUI 3 UI framework (Windows App SDK 1.8.3), .NET 9.0.12 runtime, XAML-based declarative UI, MVVM pattern support.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Three-layer architecture: GoogleCalendarManagement (WinUI 3 UI), GoogleCalendarManagement.Core (Business Logic), GoogleCalendarManagement.Data (Data Access). Story 1.1 creates main UI project with folder structure: Models, Views, ViewModels, Services, Data, plus separate Tests project.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: .NET 9.0.12, Windows App SDK 1.8.3, C# 13, WinUI 3 with XAML, MVVM with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm. Key packages: Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection, Entity Framework Core 9.0.12, xUnit 2.x, Moq 4.x, FluentAssertions 6.x.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: One class per file, file name = class name. Namespaces follow folder structure. PascalCase for classes/methods, _camelCase for private fields. XAML controls use PascalCase with type suffix. Tests co-located by namespace.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Configure DI in App.xaml.cs. AddSingleton for app-level state (Logger), AddScoped for per-operation services, AddTransient for stateless algorithms. DbContext: Scoped. Use IHttpClientFactory for all HTTP services.

artifact.title: Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Test project organization: Unit/ and Integration/ folders. xUnit test runner, Moq for mocking, FluentAssertions for assertions. Algorithms require 100% unit test coverage. Services use integration tests with in-memory SQLite.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: NFR-M1: Code quality with dependency injection for testability. NFR-M2: Test project created in Story 1.1. NFR-M4: Comprehensive documentation required.

artifact.title: UX Design Specification
artifact.snippet: Application window: 1024x768 default size, min 800x600. Uses Fluent Design System with WinUI 3 controls. Resizable window with min-width/min-height constraints.

artifact.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Create .NET 9 WinUI 3 desktop application with folder organization, dependency injection, editorconfig, gitignore. Testing framework (xUnit, FluentAssertions, Moq) from first story. Solution contains 4 projects: main WinUI 3 app, Core class library, Data class library, Tests project.

artifact.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Main project: Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK 1.8.3, Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection 9.0.x, CommunityToolkit.Mvvm 8.3.2. Test project: xUnit 2.9.2, xunit.runner.visualstudio 2.8.2, Moq 4.20.72, FluentAssertions 6.12.2, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.11.1.

artifact.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: 4 projects in solution: GoogleCalendarManagement (WinUI 3 executable), GoogleCalendarManagement.Core (Class library .NET 9), GoogleCalendarManagement.Data (Class library .NET 9), GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests (xUnit test project .NET 9). Views/, ViewModels/, Services/, Repositories/, Entities/, Configurations/ folders must exist.

artifact.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: Unit tests for dependency injection: ServiceProvider should resolve DbContext and ILogger. Manual tests: launch application, verify window appears, verify folder structure, build solution with 0 errors. Sample smoke test required in SmokeTests.cs.

code.note: No existing code - this is the first story creating the project foundation

package.name: Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK
package.purpose: WinUI 3 framework and Windows App SDK

architectural.constraint: MUST use .NET 9.0.12 runtime with target framework net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 or later
MUST use Windows App SDK 1.8.3 (not just 1.5+)
MUST use WinUI 3 for UI framework with XAML
MUST follow MVVM pattern - prepare folder structure for ViewModels from start
MUST implement dependency injection from Story 1.1 using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection 9.0.x
MUST create test project in Story 1.1, not added later - critical for TDD approach
MUST create exactly 4 projects: GoogleCalendarManagement (WinUI 3 exe), GoogleCalendarManagement.Core (class lib), GoogleCalendarManagement.Data (class lib), GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests (xUnit)
MUST create folders: Views/, ViewModels/, Services/, Repositories/, Entities/, Configurations/

projectStructure.constraint: One class per file, file name must match class name
Folder structure must follow: Models, Views, ViewModels, Services, Data
Tests must be co-located by namespace (e.g., Services/ → Tests/Unit/Services/)
No hard-coded paths - all relative or AppData based

naming.constraint: XAML Controls: PascalCase with type suffix (e.g., MainCalendarView, PublishButton)

testing.constraint: MUST use xUnit 2.x as test runner
Test project must be .NET 9 targeting same framework as main app
Create /Unit and /Integration folders in test project structure
Sample smoke test required to verify test framework works

configuration.constraint: MUST create .editorconfig with C# formatting rules (4 spaces, UTF-8, CRLF for Windows)
MUST create .gitignore for .NET, Visual Studio, WinUI 3 (bin/, obj/, .vs/, data/)

qualityStandards.constraint: NFR-M1: Code quality - dependency injection for testability
NFR-M2: Testing framework established in first story
Application must compile with no errors in both Debug and Release configurations
Hot reload should be enabled for faster development iterations

interface.name: IServiceCollection (DI Container)
interface.signature: Standard .NET DI container configured in App.xaml.cs OnLaunched method
interface.usage: Register services using AddSingleton, AddScoped, AddTransient methods

interface.name: MainWindow
interface.signature: Standard WinUI 3 window with XAML layout and code-behind
interface.usage: Entry point window, configured with 1024x768 default size, min 800x600, placeholder UI

interfaces.note: Future stories will define business logic interfaces - this story establishes infrastructure only

tests.standards: Test project GoogleCalendarManagement.Tests uses xUnit 2.x as test runner with Moq 4.x for mocking and FluentAssertions 6.x for assertions. Tests are organized into /Unit and /Integration folders, co-located by namespace with source code. Story 1.1 requires a smoke test to verify the test framework is properly configured. Future stories will add meaningful tests for services, algorithms, and data access. Algorithms require 100% unit test coverage. Services use integration tests with in-memory SQLite. UI testing is manual (WinUI 3 automated UI testing is complex).

test.name: CanCreateMainWindow_ReturnsTrue
test.description: Smoke test to verify xUnit test framework is configured correctly. Tests that a simple assertion passes using FluentAssertions syntax.

test.name: TestExplorer_DiscoversTests
test.description: Verify Visual Studio Test Explorer can discover and list the smoke test.
test.verification: Manual verification in Visual Studio Test Explorer

test.name: FluentAssertions_SyntaxWorks
test.description: Verify FluentAssertions package is installed and syntax works correctly.

test.name: Moq_PackageAvailable
test.description: Verify Moq package is installed and available for future mocking needs.
test.note: No actual mocking in Story 1.1, just verify package availability

futureTests.note: Story 1.2 will add DbContext instantiation tests
Story 1.3 will add database schema validation tests
Epic 2+ will add service layer tests with Moq mocking
Epic 3+ will add algorithm tests requiring 100% coverage
